title: Your Agent Failed in Prod. Good Luck Reproducing It. - Tisha Chawla & Susheem Koul, Microsoft
summary: The record and replay pattern captures every model invocation, tool execution, memory access, and state transition into an append-only event log to enable deterministic replay of failed agent executions in production. This approach moves beyond state recovery and API mocking to provide engineers with the ability to step through execution traces line by line for root cause analysis. The pattern brings low-level systems debugging philosophy (similar to Mozilla rr) into autonomous agent loops to address the challenge of non-deterministic failures that are difficult to reproduce.
writeup: This talk presents a record and replay pattern for debugging agent failures in production, capturing every model invocation, tool execution, memory access, and state transition into an append-only event log so a failed run can be replayed deterministically. It matters because non-deterministic agent failures are hard to reproduce, which blocks root cause analysis. The key takeaways: the approach goes beyond state recovery and API mocking, lets engineers step through execution traces line by line, and imports low-level systems debugging philosophy (comparable to Mozilla rr) into autonomous agent loops.
